questions.question_text: Basic materials used in the present solid state electronic devices like diode, transistor, ICs, etc are
questions.question_html: Basic materials used in the present solid state electronic devices like diode, transistor, ICs, etc are
questions.explanation: Semiconductors are materials that have electrical conductivity between conductors such as most metals and nonconductors or insulators like ceramics. Semiconductor material is used in the manufacturing of electrical components and used in electronic devices such as transistors and diodes. Semiconductors are the basic materials used in the present solid-state electronic devices like diode, transistor, ICs etc

questions.question_text: In the case of metals, the valence and conduction bands
questions.question_html: In the case of metals, the valence and conduction bands

options.text: do not overlap, energy gap is large
options.html: do not overlap, energy gap is large

options.text: do not overlap, energy gap is small
options.html: do not overlap, energy gap is small

questions.explanation: In the case of conductors, energy gap is absent or in other words conduction band, and valence band overlaps each other.

questions.question_text: In the case of semiconductors, the valence and conduction bands
questions.question_html: In the case of semiconductors, the valence and conduction bands

options.text: do not overlap, energy gap is large
options.html: do not overlap, energy gap is large

options.text: do not overlap, energy gap is small
options.html: do not overlap, energy gap is small

questions.correct_option_text: do not overlap, energy gap is small
questions.explanation: Semiconductors have an energy gap that is in between that of conductors and insulators. This gap is typically more or less 1 eV.

questions.question_text: A piece of copper and another of germanium are cooled from room temperature to 77K. The resistance of:
questions.question_html: A piece of copper and another of germanium are cooled from room temperature to 77K. The resistance of:

options.text: copper strip increases and that of germanium decreases
options.html: copper strip increases and that of germanium decreases

options.text: copper strip decreases and that of germanium increases
options.html: copper strip decreases and that of germanium increases

questions.correct_option_text: copper strip decreases and that of germanium increases
questions.explanation: The resistance of copper (a conductor) decreases and that of germanium (a semiconductor) increases on decrease of temperature.

questions.question_text: In a pure or intrinsic semiconductor, valence band holes and conduction-band electrons are always
questions.question_html: In a pure or intrinsic semiconductor, valence band holes and conduction-band electrons are always

options.text: such that number of electrons is greater than the number of holes
options.html: such that number of electrons is greater than the number of holes

options.text: such that number of holes is greater than the number of electrons
options.html: such that number of holes is greater than the number of electrons

questions.explanation: An intrinsic semiconductor also called an undoped semiconductor is a pure semiconductor without any significant dopant species present. In intrinsic semiconductors, the number of excited electrons and the number of holes are equal, n e = n h

questions.question_text: Doping of semiconductor is the process of
questions.question_html: Doping of semiconductor is the process of
questions.explanation: Doping is the process of adding impurities to intrinsic semiconductors to increase their conductivity.

questions.question_text: Motion of hole is a convenient way of describing
questions.question_html: Motion of hole is a convenient way of describing

options.text: the actual motion of energy gap
options.html: the actual motion of energy gap

options.text: the actual motion of free electrons
options.html: the actual motion of free electrons

options.text: the actual motion of bound electrons
options.html: the actual motion of bound electrons

options.text: the actual motion of conduction electrons
options.html: the actual motion of conduction electrons

questions.correct_option_text: the actual motion of bound electrons
questions.explanation: The motion of the hole is a convenient way of describing the actual motion of bound electrons, whenever there is an empty bond anywhere in the crystal.

questions.question_text: The total current in a semiconductor is
questions.question_html: The total current in a semiconductor is

options.text: the difference of electron and hole currents
options.html: the difference of electron and hole currents

options.text: the sum of electron and hole currents
options.html: the sum of electron and hole currents

questions.correct_option_text: the sum of electron and hole currents
questions.explanation: In a semiconductor electric current is carried by both electrons and holes. The total current in intrinsic semiconductor is the sum of hole and electron current.

questions.question_text: In n-type semiconductor, the fifth electron

options.text: needs high energy to be set free
options.html: needs high energy to be set free

options.text: needs low energy to be set free
options.html: needs low energy to be set free

questions.correct_option_text: needs low energy to be set free
questions.explanation: Suppose, in any pure or intrinsic germanium or silicon semiconductor any pentavalent impurity is added. The pentavalent impurities are those which have atoms with five valence electrons. As soon as we add the impurity to the semiconductor, the impurity atoms will replace some of the semiconductor atoms in the crystal structure. Now four of the five valence electrons of impurity atom will involve in bonding with four neighbourhood semiconductor atoms, but the fifth one electron will not find any place to occupy. This fifth electron of the impurity atom can be made available as free electron or negative charge carrier even if a very small amount of energy is applied.

questions.question_text: When p-n junction diode is forward biased, then:
questions.question_html: When p-n junction diode is forward biased, then:

options.text: the depletion region is reduced and barrier height is increased.
options.html: the depletion region is reduced and barrier height is increased.

options.text: both the depletion region and barrier height are reduced.
options.html: both the depletion region and barrier height are reduced.

options.text: both the depletion region and barrier height are increased.
options.html: both the depletion region and barrier height are increased.

options.text: the depletion region is widened and barrier height is reduced.
options.html: the depletion region is widened and barrier height is reduced.

questions.correct_option_text: both the depletion region and barrier height are reduced.
questions.explanation: When the p-n junction is forward biased, the majority carriers in each section (holes in p-section and electrons in n-section) cross to the other side. As a result of recombination of the holes and electrons, the width of the depletion region gets reduced.

questions.question_text: In n-type semiconductor majority carriers and minority carriers are respectively
questions.question_html: In n-type semiconductor majority carriers and minority carriers are respectively
questions.explanation: N-type semiconductors have a larger electron concentration than hole concentration. The term n-type comes from the negative charge of the electron. In n-type semiconductors, electrons are the majority carriers and holes are the minority carriers. ​​​​​​​ N-type semiconductors are created by doping an intrinsic semiconductor with donor impurities (or doping a p-type semiconductor as done in the making of CMOS chips). A common dopant for n-type silicon is phosphorus.

questions.question_text: In p-type semiconductor, the dopant is
questions.explanation: In p-type semiconductors, holes are the majority carriers and electrons are the minority carriers. p-type semiconductors are created by doping an intrinsic semiconductor with acceptor impurities.

Number density of valence band holes (n h ) in p-type semiconductor is approximately equal to that of the acceptor atoms (N a ) and is very large as compared to the number density of conduction band electrons (n e ). Thus, n h {tex}\approx{/tex} N a >> n e

questions.question_text: The electron and hole concentration in a semiconductor in thermal equilibrium is given by
questions.question_html: The electron and hole concentration in a semiconductor in thermal equilibrium is given by
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, the product of the majority and minority carrier concentration is a constant, and this is mathematically expressed by the Law of Mass Action, {tex}{n_e}{n_h} = n_i^2{/tex} where n i is the intrinsic carrier concentration and n e and n h are the electron and hole equilibrium carrier concentrations.

questions.question_text: Diffusion in a p-n junction is due to
questions.question_html: Diffusion in a p-n junction is due to
questions.explanation: In n-type semiconductor, the concentration of electrons is more compared to the concentration of holes. Similarly, in p-type semiconductor, the concentration of holes is more compared to the concentration of electrons. In the formation of the p-n junction, due to the concentration gradient across the 'p' and the 'n' sides, the electrons diffuse from 'n' region to 'p' region and the holes diffuse from p region to n region.
